operational_metrics._comment_period_check: 676.0 uptime + 68.0 downtime = 744.0 = 31 days × 24 hrs ✓. TMMC runs 3-shift weekdays (22 days × 24 hrs = 528 hrs) plus 4 Saturday half-shifts (4 × 10 hrs = 40 hrs) plus standby = 568 scheduled hrs. 68 hrs downtime covers charging gaps and 2 planned maintenance windows.
operational_metrics._comment_recovery: Mean 63.8 s to return to autonomous operation. 55% of incidents required physical human assist — higher than pure pick-and-place because mixed-traffic navigation recoveries are harder to self-resolve.

task_metrics._comment_utilisation: Active task-seconds = 3900 × 480 = 1,872,000 s = 520 hrs. 520 / 676 uptime = 76.9% task utilisation. Remaining 23.1% is inter-task travel standby and part-staging wait at supermarket — normal for parts-delivery workflows.

ai_performance._comment_drift: drift_index 0.083 is nominal (0.00–0.10 band). Slightly above Figure 02 BMW benchmark (0.062) owing to higher scene diversity in TMMC mixed-traffic aisles vs structured BMW body-shop stations.

stability_safety._comment_fall: 1 fall in 676 uptime hrs = 0.148 falls per 100 hrs. Self-recovery, no damage, no bystander contact (severity 1.5/10). Floor-level tote misalignment caused momentary loss of stance stability.
stability_safety._comment_compliance: ISO 13482 is false — correctly N/A for an industrial parts-delivery humanoid. Applicable standards are ISO 10218 (industrial robots) and ISO/TS 15066 (collaborative operation). Both satisfied.

energy_metrics._comment_energy: 676 uptime_hrs × 1.80 kW avg draw = 1,216.8 kWh → 1217 kWh. Ontario industrial rate CAD $0.065/kWh (Global Adjustment included). Period energy cost = 1217 × 0.065 = CAD $79.1. energy_cost_per_task (CAD) = 79.1 / 3549 completed = $0.022. NOTE: field named '_usd' but value in CAD per financial_metrics.currency_code.

maintenance_metrics._comment_maintenance_cost: Period cost (CAD) = CAD 18,000/yr × (31/365) = CAD $1,529. Two scheduled inspections plus one opportunistic seal replacement.
maintenance_metrics._comment_lifetime_hours: Deployed 2025-09-01; avg uptime 676/31 = 21.81 hrs/day × 212 days elapsed = 4,625 hrs modelled. Stored 4,580 accounting for lower-intensity ramp-up period in Sept 2025.

financial_metrics._comment_currency: All monetary values in Canadian Dollars (ISO 4217: CAD). currency_code governs all monetary fields in this object.
financial_metrics._comment_am_roi: AM-ROI = [(R + HLE×DF) × TSR × AS] / TCO_annual. R=0 (internal deployment). Numerator = (0 + 72000×0.80) × 0.910 × 0.87 = 57600 × 0.910 × 0.87 = 57600 × 0.7917 = CAD $45,601.92. AM-ROI = 45601.92 / 54000 = 0.8445 → stored 0.844. Below 1.0 expected in Y1 per schema; lower capital cost vs BMW Figure 02 produces better Y1 ratio despite lower TSR.
financial_metrics._comment_tco: TCO_annual (CAD) = P/L + C_energy + C_maint + C_operator + C_insurance + C_integration = (150000÷7=21428.57) + 950 + 18000 + 7500 + 3750 + 2371.43 = CAD $54,000.00 ✓. C_insurance = CAD $3750 = 2.5% × 150000, consistent with MRS 0.787 standard tier (schema band: 2.0–3.5% of P annually).
financial_metrics._comment_robot_age: 2026-03-31 minus 2025-09-01: Sept(30)+Oct(31)+Nov(30)+Dec(31)+Jan(31)+Feb(28)+Mar(31) = 212 days → rapid_initial phase (0–730 days) ✓. depreciation_rate_annual 0.23 within valid rapid_initial range 0.20–0.25 ✓.
financial_metrics._comment_hle: HLE = CAD $26.50/hr × 1.35 employer burden multiplier × 2012 hrs/yr = CAD $72,063 → rounded to CAD $72,000. Reflects Ontario automotive assembly rate including payroll taxes, WSIB, health benefits, and overhead.
financial_metrics._comment_cost_per_task: cost_per_task = TCO_annual / tasks_completed_annual = 54000 / (3549 × 365÷31) = 54000 / 41,793 = CAD $1.292. Higher than pick-and-place robots because 8-minute delivery cycles produce far fewer tasks per year than 2.5-minute cycles.
financial_metrics._comment_rsv: RSV = P×(1−cum_dep)×condition_factor×model_factor. cum_dep=0.23×(212÷365)=0.23×0.58082=0.13359. (1−0.13359)=0.86641. condition_factor=(TSR×0.6)+(AS×0.4)=(0.910×0.6)+(0.87×0.4)=0.546+0.348=0.894. model_factor=0.70 (Digit under active Agility support; nascent humanoid resale market). RSV=150000×0.86641×0.894×0.70=150000×0.54220=CAD $81,330.
financial_metrics._comment_raas: All 676 uptime hours qualify for billing: TSR 0.910 ≥ contracted 0.87 AND AS 0.87 ≥ contracted 0.82. raas_rate_per_hour reflects Agility Robotics RaaS commercial rate to TMMC in CAD.
financial_metrics._comment_payback: Net_Value_annual = (0 + 72000×0.80)×0.910×0.87 = CAD $45,601.92. TCO_excl_capital = 54000 − 21428.57 = CAD $32,571.43. annual_surplus = 45601.92 − 32571.43 = CAD $13,030.49. monthly_surplus = 13030.49÷12 = CAD $1,085.87. months_elapsed = 212÷30.4167 = 6.97. cum_surplus = 6.97 × 1085.87 = CAD $7,568. payback_remaining = (150000 − 7568) ÷ 1085.87 = 131.2 months ≈ 10.9 yrs. Payback extends beyond 7-yr lifespan at current AM-ROI; viable if AS and TSR improve as Agility software matures.

environment._comment_facility: Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada (TMMC), Cambridge plant. Digit deployed in Parts Logistics — Zone 7 (body shop supermarket to trim-line delivery aisles). Mixed traffic with human operators, electric tow-trains, and Toyota AGV fleet.

mars_risk_score._comment_mtbi_norm: MTBI_norm = min(112.67 ÷ 200_automotive_benchmark, 1.0) = min(0.56335, 1.0) = 0.563. Benchmark 200 hrs per schema automotive_manufacturing reference.

raas_sla._comment_sla: All six metrics compliant. Uptime ratio 676÷744=0.9086 ≥ contracted 0.88. TMMC 3-shift pattern enables this higher floor vs typical 2-shift deployments. No breaches this period.
raas_sla._comment_thresholds: Contracted minimums set conservatively at inception (Sept 2025) reflecting Digit early-deployment risk tolerance. TSR floor 0.87 vs BMW SPB 0.88; AS floor 0.82 vs BMW SPB 0.85 — recognises logistics navigation complexity vs structured pick-and-place.
